reviews.review_description: I tried to download Liv mobile on my iPhone for quest 3, but the app no ​​longer exists, apparently the Beta phase has ended. a shame since it is the app that worked best for mixed reality. I give it three stars because it gave me a lot of fun and I'm sad that it ended forever on mobile.

reviews.review_title: Exciting new way of shooting mr
reviews.review_description:
I short my first mixed reality video today and a part from small bit of lag (due to poor WiFi) the video came out very well!
Sync was at times a little out, and sabers were attached to the back of my hands( not really a LIV problem I assume) but for a beta, I’m very impressed. Ran completely on standalone vr btw!

Worth a try for sure.

Simply won’t connect. Yes both phone and Quest 2 headset are running the app on the same WiFi. And then in the headset there is the “problems connecting?” Prompt that you can’t even click or do anything with. Really wish it worked or wish there was a Mac alternative for laptops.

reviews.review_title: Finally got Mixed reality to work
reviews.review_description:
Ovver the years i  was never able to get Mixed reality to work.  Tried it on my PC, tried the original Liv Ios app, and that one app by that indie developer.  

I did not think this day would ever come.

Thank you

developer_response.body:
Congrats!! We are very excited to hear this. We have lots more features coming soon and we will certainly make it even easier to setup.

If you need some help getting setup on your PC, come chat with us at https://liv.chat/. We have a Quest solution as well as PCVR and we'd love to help you out there too.

reviews.review_title: Best tool to sell your friends on VR
reviews.review_description: Would love to see this work on way more apps. But works great for Beat saber.
I added this but it's not appearing in my Quest 2 apps, installed or not. Wish we could add it from inside the headset. What am I doing wrong?
I ran into a tiny issue with my download, put in a help ticket, and heard back from Liv in less than 30 minutes with a quick fix!
It never connects

Its not confortabe have to exit, start the game and then back to the phone to start again... but it works fine and I have been able to make livestreams without any problem at all ! @alejo_vrit for reels with some results.

I’ve tried this from multiple angles and various ways of starting it up. I can only ever see the very top of the gameplay area and that’s if I reset my view behind me. The setup runs through perfectly fine, the controllers align and I can see within the app perfectly but when I start the game I see nothing but myself and a black background.

Reseted phone;router,headset. 
Never works

Perfect to get others an impression of what I'm programming for VR. It even tracks the iPhone. Absolutely fantastic, can't wait to see how it will improve even more over time!

Good job guys

It says it works for the quest, however it fails to stay connected after leaving my app on oculus and when I load up a supported game, it says on my phone “Connection not found”

Not worth my time anymore trying to get it set up

developer_response.body: Sorry, you ran into this issue. We have received similar reports and have been able to resolve it by explaining the process better. It's likely a user experience problem on our side. We are working through lots of feedback and trying to address it all. Please come over and chat with us on Discord (liv.chat).  We'd love to help you get setup!

reviews.review_description: This seems to have potential. However, I feel like there needs to be a comprehensive, step-by-step tutorial on how apps such as this facilitate these incredibly complex technical processes in a way normal people can understand. Between all the tutorials by indie YouTubers and the vague information posted on the LIV blog, it results in mass confusion. So at the end of the day, I'm still not sure if I need to connect my phone to make this work, since it's advertised as a mobile app, if OBS is required, or exactly what I need to do maximize its potential. More importantly and frustratingly, I still have no idea of what's the way for me personally to get up and running with mixed reality.

reviews.review_title: i like about using VRChat With LIV
reviews.review_description: it's cool.

I’ve been wanting to do something like this for a while and it seems that it works for some other people. But, I’ve been having a lot of issues with it. The tracking seems to be off by a bit but I think it’s just me messing it up. What I’m having the most problems with are not seeing myself in game when looking at the preview and, when I open up Beat Saber (with mrc on) the game freezes and the preview on my computer look at the sky. I’m aware that the app is in beta and isn’t fully worked out yet but I have a feeling that most of the problems I’m having are my fault. I am exited on how this could work in the future!
Good app in Beta, you need to work harder for Oculus Quest Tools.

reviews.review_title: It’s okay but far from perfect
reviews.review_description: Don't get me wrong, it works but it's hard to calibrate the controllers.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback. We have heard lots of comments about how important it is to get a good calibration and we are committed to making it very easy. We have lots of improvements coming soon.

reviews.review_description: Every time I open the app and on my vr it won’t connect and yes there on the same connection

reviews.review_title: How do I get it on my phone
reviews.review_description: How do I get it on my phone

developer_response.body:
We are currently only available on iPhone (although Android coming soon) and (for now) only available via TestFlight.  The easiest place to find links is at https://www.liv.tv/mobile.

We are currently in the process of adding a more informative landing page on LIV Connect that gives you this information.  Sorry about the confusion.

If you have any issues (or if you just wanna talk about Mixed Reality), come chat with us at https://liv.chat.
